guest_letters.title: Send your articles to the East of Indus newspaper
guest_letters.body: By adding the prefix !eastofindus to your post, and writing your title after that, you can send an article through induschan, which will be published on iac and displayed in the next edition of the newspaper, in the morning or the evening! There is no censorship, there is quality control, and there is a 50 word minimum, send your writing!!!
guest_letters.name: India Abstract Culture

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board early, bringing scattered showers of slurs and insults. The 'Gobarida edition' comment sparked a squall of replies, dominating the morning discourse.

A heat wave of racial fetishization developed later, fueled by anon's fascination with 'gori mem' and interracial scenarios. This brought a burst of related comments and questions.

A cold front of incel anxiety moved in by evening, marked by a series of isolated posts seeking female interaction and friends.

Meanwhile, a high-pressure ridge of praise for a mother of five led to a brief but intense downpour of caste and racial remarks.

articles.title: Anon Sparks Thread with 'Gobarida Edition' Comment
articles.body:
Anon initiated a thread with the comment 'Gobarida edition' on May 15, 2026. The post was vague, prompting several respondents to seek clarification.

Anon suggested that 'Gobarida Edition' might refer to a special edition related to biogas or renewable energy, a brand or product name, or a specific edition of a book or publication.

Another possibility considered was that it could be related to a language or dialect. The conversation quickly diverged into discussions about technology and relationships.

One anon expressed desire for a 'robo waifu,' a robotic partner, and contrasted this with the idea of a '3D Randi,' a term used to describe women in a derogatory manner.

This respondent stated that they would prefer a robotic partner over human women, who they described as unfit to be mothers. Other anons engaged in conversations about social dynamics, with one expressing disdain for what they perceived as low-IQ individuals.

This respondent claimed that women from 'good enough families and education' were more desirable to engage with. They also stated a preference for 2D anime-style characters over 3D representations.

The thread also included discussions about varna sankara, a concept related to social hierarchy, and fertility rates. One anon fantasized about drastically altering the male-to-female ratio and reducing the population.

Another suggested that they would marry a woman from a certain social group if varna sankara were not a problem.

Throughout the thread, anons exchanged views on topics ranging from technology and relationships to social hierarchy and demographics, often using derogatory language and expressing extreme views.

articles.title: Anon expresses fascination with 'gori mem' and interracial scenarios
articles.body:
Anon started the conversation with a post saying 'Gorimem'. One respondent wrote that they don't understand the appeal of 'gori mem', describing them as 'cheap Randis in NCR'. Another poster expressed a similar sentiment, stating that they 'never understood gori mem glaze'.

Some anons discussed interracial scenarios, with one writing about enjoying non-nude interracial kissing compilations. Another anon mentioned a kink involving watching someone else's partner engage in sexual activity with a Black man, specifically mentioning 'BBC'.

The conversation also involved some anons accusing others of being homosexual or 'faggot' due to their expressed interests.

One poster stated that liking another man's genitalia is gay, prompting a response about not making assumptions about someone's sexual orientation based on their kink. The discussion included various derogatory terms and slurs, particularly targeting people of Indian and African descent.

Some anons used terms like 'gobar pajeeta', 'dalit', and 'Bantu' in a derogatory manner. The conversation remained centered around these topics, with anons sharing their thoughts and reactions to the initial post and subsequent replies.

articles.title: Anon Claims Caste-Based Rape Statistics Are Proof Of 'UC' Inferiority
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread with a Good Night message to fellow posters. Anon then claimed that certain castes, such as OBC, have a history of criminal activity, citing specific individuals and their caste affiliations.

This individual listed several names, asserting they belonged to the OBC caste.

Another poster responded by making derogatory comments about Dalits, stating they look 'dysgenic' and are 'historically stupid.' This individual claimed that if a Dalit rapes someone, their genes improve, but if someone marries a Dalit woman, their genes degrade.

A respondent brought up a scenario involving a girl who was raped, suggesting that the perpetrator was from a lower caste and that the victim's caste background made her more susceptible to such incidents.

Anon also joked about the perceived double standard in reactions to rape cases, noting that when a Muslim girl is raped, it becomes international news, but when a 'UC' individual is raped, nobody cares.

A later poster expressed support for violent actions against certain groups, stating they 'do not deserve any single human emotion.' Anon engaged in caste-based discussions, arguing that certain groups, like Gujjars, seek validation from others, such as Jatts.

Anon also referenced a case where the perpetrator was allegedly from a different caste, sparking debate about the role of caste in these incidents.

articles.title: Anon Seeks Friends, Female Interaction
articles.body:
Anon started the thread saying they need friends. They mentioned having friends, but finding them useless and in need of female interaction.

One respondent suggested starting with less attractive women to gain experience, then moving to more attractive ones.

Another Anon shared their approach to social interactions, emphasizing the importance of understanding the listener and having confidence.

Some anons discussed their own struggles with social interactions, including feeling humiliated and having low "izzat points." One Anon expressed a desire for a family and children, while another mentioned wanting to make friends and a girlfriend in college.

articles.title: Anon Praises Mother of Five, Sparks Caste and Racial Remarks
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread, calling a mother of five "Pajeetas moot degi ye sab karne mein without getting thulthul." Anon replied, saying they could understand liking "white women" but found the "forced Jeets hate" to be "faggot coded." Another poster joked that a man would rather marry his daughter to someone from his caste than a "good looking pajeet from another caste," let alone a Muslim.

Anon also exchanged insults with others, with one saying they "don't deserve pretty wives" and another calling them "bhangi lc and fat ass." One respondent suggested that upper castes can "mix blood" with certain groups in Pakistan.
